mechanisms.description: For public apps used by multiple Calendly users
mechanisms.refresh_token: Rotating — does not expire until used

authentication.subscription_requirements: Webhook API requires paid premium subscription and above. API access tied to user role (Owner/Admin).

rate_limits.description: Rate limits applied per token. Specific limits not publicly documented (refer to API docs for current values).
rate_limits.notes: Calendly has recently introduced a Scheduling API (no redirects/iframes) with its own rate limits.

webhooks.requires: Paid premium subscription or above. Owner/Admin role.

error_dictionary.solution: Verify token is valid. Personal access tokens: check developer settings. OAuth: token expires after 2 hours, use refresh token.

causes: Trying to access data outside user's organization
error_dictionary.solution: Check user role and subscription. Webhooks require paid premium subscription.
Verify UUID format and resource existence. Check that event is within accessible date range.
Check for existing resources before creating duplicates.
Check required fields and data types. Event type UUIDs must be valid.
Implement exponential backoff. Check Retry-After header if provided.

community_constraints.issue: Webhook API requires paid subscription — not available for free tier
community_constraints.workaround: Use polling with GET /scheduled_events for free tier. But polling increases API consumption. Upgrade to premium for webhook access.
Implement conservative client-side rate limiting. Start with 10 req/min and adjust based on 429 responses. Use exponential backoff with jitter.

use_cases: Sync scheduled meetings to CRM events
Create HubSpot contacts/leads from Calendly invitees
Send Slack notifications when meetings are booked/canceled
Automate follow-up sequences after scheduled events
